texto: There is something lamentable, degrading, and almost insane in pursuing the visionary schemes of past ages with dogged determination, in paths of learning which have been investigated by superior minds, and with which such adventurous persons are totally unacquainted. The history of Perpetual Motion is a history of the fool-hardiness of either half-learned, or totally ignorant persons.
explicacao: Citação de Henry Dircks sobre temas ligados a sabedoria. “There is something lamentable, degrading, and almost insane in pursuing the visionary schemes of …” articula sabedoria, foolishness, physics em registro de sabedoria, com relevância para contextos como physics.

texto: Of what use can our labour be, when the labours we record are in themselves entirely useless?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Henry Dircks que provoca temas ligados a trabalho. Ao questionar “Of what use can our labour be, when the labours we record are in themselves entirely useless?”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre trabalho, value, work, com foco em trabalho e work.
